/works/croatia-autentica.title: Croatia Autentica - GFIGUEIREDO
/works/croatia-autentica.description: Portfolio of Gonçalo Figueiredo. Senior Product Designer with 5+ years in design systems, accessibility, and AI workflows. 80+ projects across 20+ countries.
p: Croatia Autentica is a boutique luxury travel agency creating fully escorted, tailor-made journeys across Croatia. The project focused on designing a premium digital experience that captures the emotion of travel while clearly communicating the agency's highly personalised approach, transforming the website into both an inspirational showcase and a trusted consultation platform.

The challenge was shifting users' perception from booking a tour to planning a completely bespoke journey. Rather than presenting static travel packages, the experience was designed around storytelling, helping visitors imagine what travelling with Croatia Autentica feels like before discussing an itinerary.

The website combines cinematic visuals, immersive interactions, and carefully structured content to guide users through signature tours, destinations, travel styles, and authentic local experiences. Throughout the journey, the emphasis remains on what makes the agency different: every itinerary is privately escorted, fully customised, and personally curated from the first consultation through the final day of travel.
Families wanting fully planned travel experiences.
Travellers looking for authentic Croatian culture.
International visitors who value personalised service over package tourism.
Premium travellers looking for concierge-style journeys.

This project reminded me that luxury isn't defined by visual style alone, it's created through the entire user experience. Every interaction needed to reinforce trust, exclusivity, and personal attention while keeping the planning process effortless.

Designing Croatia Autentica meant balancing inspiration with clarity. Rich storytelling, immersive media, and elegant interactions had to work alongside practical information to help users confidently move from dreaming about Croatia to starting a conversation about their own bespoke journey.

/works/go-coaching.title: GO! Coaching - GFIGUEIREDO
/works/go-coaching.description: Portfolio of Gonçalo Figueiredo. Senior Product Designer with 5+ years in design systems, accessibility, and AI workflows. 80+ projects across 20+ countries.
p: Modern coaching for ambitious loan officers.

GO! Coaching is one of North America's leading coaching communities for loan officers, providing structured education, accountability, events, and professional development. The project involved a complete redesign of the platform, transforming its digital presence from a small coaching business into a premium industry-leading brand capable of supporting multiple programs, events, educational resources, and a growing member community.

The challenge extended far beyond improving aesthetics. The existing website no longer reflected the scale of the business or the ambition behind the brand. The objective was to reposition GO! Coaching as the modern leader in mortgage coaching, creating an experience that felt authoritative, premium, and built for long-term growth.

Rather than organizing the site around isolated services, the new experience was structured around a complete coaching ecosystem. Clear learning pathways, a five-level coaching system, community-driven content, educational resources, events, merchandise, and multiple conversion journeys were carefully connected into a single experience. The darker visual language, cinematic imagery, and stronger storytelling helped reinforce the shift from a coaching company to an industry-leading platform.
Loan officers looking to increase production and consistency.
Experienced producers ready to scale their business.
Team leaders and branch managers developing high-performing teams.
Mortgage professionals seeking accountability, coaching, and community.
This project completely changed how I think about redesigns. The goal wasn't to make an existing website look better, it was to redefine how the company presents itself to the industry.

Working through information architecture, brand positioning, user journeys, and visual direction simultaneously reinforced the importance of designing around business perception, not just interface design. Seeing the final product successfully communicate the scale and ambition of the company made this one of the most rewarding projects I've worked on.

/works/pixelab.title: Pixelab - GFIGUEIREDO
/works/pixelab.description: Portfolio of Gonçalo Figueiredo. Senior Product Designer with 5+ years in design systems, accessibility, and AI workflows. 80+ projects across 20+ countries.
p: A creator platform built around digital ownership.

Pixelab is a concept mobile platform exploring how creators can build, share, and monetize digital work within a unified ecosystem. The project focused on designing an immersive mobile experience that combines discovery, creator profiles, digital collectibles, and social interaction through a bold visual identity and carefully structured user journeys.

The goal was to create a product that felt modern, expressive, and effortless to navigate despite supporting multiple user goals. From onboarding and discovery to profile management and creator interactions, every screen was designed around reducing friction while maintaining a premium visual experience.

Rather than treating each feature as a separate flow, the interface was approached as a connected ecosystem where navigation, content hierarchy, and visual consistency work together to create an engaging experience for both creators and their audiences.

The project resulted in a complete product concept spanning onboarding, navigation, creator discovery, profile management, authentication, and marketplace-related experiences. Establishing a consistent design system and reusable UI components created a scalable foundation capable of supporting future features while maintaining a cohesive user experience.
Digital creators building an online presence.
Users discovering creative content and communities.
Collectors interested in digital assets and NFTs.
Mobile-first users who value intuitive, visually engaging products.

Pixelab gave me the opportunity to design a product from the ground up, thinking beyond individual screens and focusing on how an entire ecosystem should feel. It reinforced the importance of creating scalable design systems, maintaining consistency across complex user flows, and balancing bold visual direction with intuitive usability.

/works/volta-esim.title: Volta eSIM - GFIGUEIREDO
/works/volta-esim.description: Portfolio of Gonçalo Figueiredo. Senior Product Designer with 5+ years in design systems, accessibility, and AI workflows. 80+ projects across 20+ countries.
p: Volta eSIM is a travel connectivity platform designed to help users quickly find, purchase, and manage destination-based eSIM plans. The project focused on creating a clear, conversion-led website and authenticated user experience, combining destination search, reusable product templates, cashback rewards, referral systems, affiliate tools, and account management into one scalable digital platform.

The main challenge was designing a product that felt simple on the surface while supporting a fairly complex ecosystem behind it. Users needed to find a travel destination quickly, compare fixed data packages, understand how eSIMs work, and purchase without unnecessary steps. At the same time, the platform needed to support cashback, referrals, subscriptions, affiliate earnings, support flows, device compatibility, billing, and post-purchase eSIM management.

The UX approach was built around reducing decision fatigue. Instead of overwhelming users with too many plan types or generic travel content, the experience prioritised fast search, clear product pages, reusable templates, simple CTAs, and contextual guidance at the right moments. Each flow was designed to help users move from travel intent to purchase with confidence.
Travellers looking for affordable mobile data before or during a trip.
Digital nomads and frequent travellers managing multiple destinations.
Users comparing roaming alternatives before travelling abroad.
Influencers and creators looking for recurring affiliate income.
Existing customers returning to top up, subscribe, or manage active eSIMs.

DPG reinforced how design shapes perception long before a client makes contact. Creating a premium experience wasn't about adding more, it was about knowing what to remove. Every decision, from layout and motion to typography and imagery, was made to communicate confidence, craftsmanship, and trust, allowing the quality of Dapper's work to take centre stage.

/works/dpg.title: Dapper Property Group - GFIGUEIREDO
/works/dpg.description: Portfolio of Gonçalo Figueiredo. Senior Product Designer with 5+ years in design systems, accessibility, and AI workflows. 80+ projects across 20+ countries.
p: Premium property development in the Algarve.

Dapper Property Group is a luxury property development company specialising in high-end renovations, bespoke construction, and investment opportunities across the Algarve. The project focused on repositioning the brand through a premium digital experience that showcases craftsmanship, reinforces trust, and presents every development as an investment opportunity rather than simply another construction project.

The existing website no longer reflected the quality of the company's work. While Dapper delivered exceptional projects, its digital presence lacked the sophistication needed to attract premium clients and investors.

The redesign focused on elevating perception through minimal layouts, stronger visual storytelling, and a carefully curated portfolio experience. Instead of treating projects as a gallery, the website presents each development as a story, highlighting the design process, quality of execution, and attention to detail. Every interaction was designed to communicate confidence, craftsmanship, and long-term value while making it effortless for prospective clients to explore services and enquire.

The redesign established a scalable digital foundation that better represents the maturity of the GO! Coaching brand. The new information architecture simplifies navigation across coaching programs, events, educational content, and community features while creating stronger conversion opportunities throughout the site.

Beyond launching a new visual identity, the project introduced reusable design patterns, clearer program positioning, improved storytelling, and a platform capable of evolving alongside the company's continued growth.
Homeowners planning premium renovations or bespoke homes.
Property investors seeking high-quality development opportunities.
International buyers relocating or investing in Portugal.
Design-conscious clients looking for a full turn-key construction partner.

This project reinforced how important perception is within luxury markets. The challenge wasn't adding more information, it was creating confidence through restraint. Every design decision was made to let the quality of the work speak for itself, using typography, imagery, motion, and spacing to communicate craftsmanship before a single conversation takes place.

Designing for a premium audience also meant balancing emotion with usability. The result is an experience that feels elegant and aspirational while remaining practical for users exploring services, reviewing projects, or making an enquiry.

/works/siscog.title: SISCOG - GFIGUEIREDO
/works/siscog.description: Portfolio of Gonçalo Figueiredo. Senior Product Designer with 5+ years in design systems, accessibility, and AI workflows. 80+ projects across 20+ countries.
p: SISCOG develops optimisation software used by railway operators worldwide to plan, schedule, and manage complex rail operations. As part of the product design team, I contributed to the redesign of multiple enterprise applications, focusing on modernising the user experience, improving accessibility, and building a scalable design system capable of supporting an extensive suite of operational tools.

Designing for railway planning is fundamentally different from designing consumer products. Users spend entire working days interacting with highly complex interfaces where speed, accuracy, and clarity directly impact operational efficiency.

Rather than redesigning isolated screens, our work focused on creating a consistent design language across the platform. We developed a comprehensive design system covering reusable components, interaction patterns, iconography, colour tokens, notifications, navigation, and accessibility guidelines, allowing different products within the ecosystem to share a unified experience while remaining flexible enough for specialised workflows.

Accessibility became one of the project's biggest priorities. We completely redesigned the application's visual themes, introducing three carefully crafted accessibility modes, including dedicated themes for users with different forms of colour vision deficiency. These themes were refined through continuous testing with colour-blind participants, ensuring critical operational information could be understood regardless of visual limitations without compromising usability or efficiency.

Working alongside developers, testers, and product stakeholders, every component was validated not only for visual consistency but also for technical feasibility, scalability, and long-term maintenance.
Transport companies operating complex railway systems.
Internal teams configuring operational planning software.

SISCOG fundamentally changed the way I approach product design. It taught me that great enterprise software isn't about making interfaces look simpler—it's about helping experts make complex decisions with confidence.

Working on accessibility at this scale was particularly rewarding. Designing and validating three complete interface themes alongside colour-blind users reinforced that accessibility should never be treated as an afterthought, but as a core part of the product experience. Combined with the challenge of building a reusable design system across multiple applications, this project shaped my understanding of scalable product design, cross-functional collaboration, and designing for real-world operational impact.

/works/lsa.title: London Sound Academy - GFIGUEIREDO
/works/lsa.description: Portfolio of Gonçalo Figueiredo. Senior Product Designer with 5+ years in design systems, accessibility, and AI workflows. 80+ projects across 20+ countries.
p: London Sound Academy, a leading electronic music academy in London, needed a stronger website system to support its DJ and music production courses. The project focused on building a bold, music-led digital presence with clearer course pathways, stronger tutor and studio positioning, and a booking-focused structure designed to turn aspiring artists into students.

The biggest challenge wasn't adding more content... it was creating clarity. Prospective students arrived with different goals, experience levels, and expectations, making it essential to simplify the decision-making process without losing the academy's energetic identity. Every page was designed to reduce friction, reinforce credibility through tutors and facilities, and guide users naturally toward booking a course or getting in touch.
Aspiring DJs and music producers exploring professional training.
Beginners looking for an accessible entry point into electronic music.
Intermediate and advanced artists seeking to develop technical skills.
International students researching London's leading music academies.

This project reinforced the importance of balancing brand expression with usability. While the visual identity needed to capture the energy of electronic music culture, the experience itself had to remain intuitive and conversion-focused. It was a valuable exercise in designing for both emotion and clarity, ensuring the brand's personality never came at the expense of user confidence.
